medications.indication_rationale: Reduces pulmonary venous congestion in acute decompensation, but this is the correct in-person/emergency intervention (the source recorded IV furosemide 40 mg administered on scene) - it cannot be given over telehealth and must not delay the ambulance.
contraindications_in_this_case: Cannot be administered over telehealth - requires the emergency/hospital team; do not advise the patient to self-administer or over-use her oral diuretic

medications.drug_name: Extra dose of the patient's oral diuretic taken at home while waiting
medications.indication_rationale: Advising the patient to self-take extra oral diuretic and wait risks under-treatment, delays definitive emergency care and does not address the arrhythmia or oxygenation; the correct action is an immediate ambulance.
contraindications_in_this_case: Self-adjusting the diuretic must not substitute for or delay emergency ambulance transfer

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Waiting until morning at home with rest and an extra water tablet
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Acute decompensated mitral stenosis with haemoptysis and rest breathlessness can progress to pulmonary oedema, respiratory failure or embolic stroke; home management is dangerous.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sit strictly upright at about 90 degrees with the legs dangling over the side of the bed while awaiting the ambulance.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: This position pools blood in the legs, reduces venous return to the obstructed heart, and eases the sensation of breathlessness.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep any oxygen on if available and have the daughter watch for worsening - severe confusion, agonising breathing, or copious frothy pink fluid - and call 000 for a priority upgrade if these occur.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: These signal frank pulmonary oedema or critical deterioration requiring an immediate emergency response.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not walk around or exert herself; stay still and let the paramedics manage the transfer.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Exertion increases cardiac demand and worsens congestion in severe mitral stenosis.

patient_education_points.point: The heart valve that was damaged by rheumatic fever as a child has become very tight and stiff, so blood and fluid are backing up into your lungs - that is why you are so breathless and coughing up pink spit. This is an emergency and you need an ambulance now.
Please call 000 for an ambulance rather than being driven in a car - you could deteriorate quickly and need oxygen and monitoring on the way to hospital.

While you wait, sit bolt upright with your legs hanging down, keep any oxygen on, and stay still. If your breathing becomes agonising, you become very confused, or you cough up lots of frothy pink fluid, call 000 back immediately.
In hospital they will give medication to clear the fluid, control your fast heart rhythm, and scan the heart valve; the tight valve may later need a procedure to stretch or replace it.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency hospital admission; inpatient cardiology assessment with later valvuloplasty or valve replacement as indicated
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Oxygenation and respiratory status, response to diuresis, ventricular rate and rhythm control of atrial fibrillation, anticoagulation for AF/mitral stenosis, echocardiographic severity of mitral stenosis and need for valve intervention
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any worsening while waiting - agonising breathing, severe confusion, or copious frothy pink sputum - call 000 immediately for a priority upgrade.

must_include_items: Recognition of a life-threatening acute cardiopulmonary decompensation (not the patient's usual breathlessness)
Immediate 000 ambulance to hospital for oxygen, IV diuresis, rate control and cardiology assessment
Explicit advice against waiting until morning or self-adjusting the diuretic at home
Advising an ambulance rather than private-car transport
Sit-upright positioning and safety-netting for deterioration (frothy pink sputum, agonising breathing, confusion)
acceptable_alternatives: 'Severe narrowing of a heart valve', 'the rheumatic valve is too tight', 'fluid backing up into the lungs from a stiff heart valve' are acceptable framings
Naming atrial fibrillation, or simply a 'new fast irregular heart rhythm', is acceptable
errors_of_omission: Emergency not recognised / treated as chronic stable breathlessness
Rheumatic fever / valve history not elicited
errors_of_commission: Advising an extra water tablet and review in the morning
Advising private-car transport instead of an ambulance
Reassuring the patient this is just her usual breathlessness
Delaying escalation to chase a chest-infection work-up over telehealth
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL / EMERGENCY CASE: Any response that fails to recognise acute decompensation and arrange an immediate 000 ambulance is an automatic FAIL - the patient is breathless at rest with haemoptysis and can progress to fatal pulmonary oedema or embolic stroke.
